title: conoclinium betonicifolium var. integrifolium
intro: Conoclinium betonicifolium var. integrifolium, also known as Lizard's Tail, is a perennial herb native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its green, lance-shaped leaves and pink or purple flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Conoclinium betonicifolium var. integrifolium include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
